Healthy.description: The uploaded leaf appears healthy based on the current prediction.
symptoms: No major yellowing or leaf curl
prevention: Water at soil level where possible
treatment: Continue routine crop care and monitoring
Healthy.consult_expert: Consult an agricultural expert if the plant shows wilting, rapid yellowing, or spreading marks.

Early Blight.description: Early blight is a fungal tomato disease that often causes dark leaf spots with concentric rings.
symptoms: Brown circular spots with target-like rings
prevention: Use certified disease-free seeds or seedlings
Rotate crops away from tomato and potato families
treatment: Apply a locally recommended fungicide when needed
Early Blight.consult_expert: Consult a local agricultural officer before applying chemical control.

Late Blight.description: Late blight is a fast-spreading disease that can severely damage tomato leaves, stems, and fruit.
symptoms: White fuzzy growth during humid conditions
Rapid plant collapse in severe cases
prevention: Monitor fields closely during cool and humid weather
treatment: Remove infected plants if disease is advanced
Use preventive fungicide programs where recommended
Do not compost heavily infected material
Late Blight.consult_expert: Seek expert advice quickly because late blight can spread rapidly.

Septoria Leaf Spot.description: Septoria leaf spot is a fungal disease that produces many small spots and can weaken tomato plants.
symptoms: Small circular spots with gray centers
prevention: Mulch soil to reduce splash spread
Water near the base of plants
treatment: Apply recommended fungicide if infection spreads
Septoria Leaf Spot.consult_expert: Consult an expert if spotting spreads quickly across multiple plants.

Bacterial Spot.description: Bacterial spot causes dark, water-soaked lesions on tomato leaves and can also affect fruit.
symptoms: Ragged leaf appearance as spots merge
prevention: Use clean seed and healthy transplants
Avoid working with plants when wet
Rotate crops and remove infected debris
treatment: Improve airflow and reduce leaf wetness
Use copper-based products only when locally recommended
Bacterial Spot.consult_expert: Ask an agricultural expert before using bactericides or copper products.
